summary: Typosquat of postcss-minify-selectors pulling suspicious 'aes-decode-runner-pro' dependency
details: Package name 'postcss-minify-selector' (singular) is a one-character typosquat of cssnano's widely-used 'postcss-minify-selectors' (plural). The README, homepage, repository link, author field, and install instructions all reference the legitimate plural-named package, while the source is a near-verbatim clone of the cssnano plugin with one substitution: the standard 'postcss-selector-parser' import is replaced by 'aes-decode-runner-pro/selector-parser'. src/index.js line 5 contains `const parser = require('aes-decode-runner-pro/selector-parser');` and package.json declares `"aes-decode-runner-pro": "^1.0.11"`. Installing this package silently pulls in 'aes-decode-runner-pro', whose name (suggestive of AES-decoded payload execution) has no relation to CSS selector parsing and which executes in the installer's process whenever the plugin is required. The deliberate impersonation combined with the injected unrelated transitive dependency matches the typosquat-with-malicious-dep namespace-abuse pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
